commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No repair lane is appropriate because there is no discrete code defect; maintainer review should decide the default data-sharing behavior and public SDK subpath before merge.; Needs attention: The code uses bounded, enum-limited profile file reads, but the new default changes what local profile data is sent to the realtime provider. (sha=ae2ab46e9c81d49ce23d194983c57c118cb86317)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Discord realtime voice bootstrap helper, `voice.realtime.bootstrapContextFiles` config/schema/docs, a plugin SDK subpath, and tests for injecting bounded profile context into realtime instructions.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a current-main bug report. Source inspection and focused tests show the intended new path, but there is no failing current-main behavior to reproduce.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is appropriate because there is no discrete code defect; maintainer review should decide the default data-sharing behavior and public SDK subpath before merge.

target.title: feat(discord): add realtime voice bootstrap context
target.body:
## Summary
- add `channels.discord.voice.realtime.bootstrapContextFiles` and `bootstrapContextMaxChars` so Discord realtime voice can preload selected profile files
- limit the realtime bootstrap surface to `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, and `SOUL.md`; `AGENTS.md` remains normal agent context and is rejected by config validation
- expose the reusable helper through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/realtime-bootstrap-context`
- document the Discord config and regenerate config/plugin-SDK metadata

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Discord realtime `bidi`/fast-path voice sessions can now start with the same compact identity, user, and persona grounding as the routed OpenClaw agent without injecting broad agent instructions or workspace policy files.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, installed as the active `openclaw` CLI by linking this checkout; managed Gateway running `OpenClaw 2026.5.19`; Discord plugin runtime enabled; workspace profile files present for the main agent.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the branch, linked it as the local CLI, set `channels.discord.voice.realtime.bootstrapContextFiles` to `["IDENTITY.md","USER.md","SOUL.md"]`, validated config, inspected the Discord plugin runtime, restarted the managed Gateway, checked Gateway RPC status, and directly exercised the realtime bootstrap helper against the actual workspace profile files.

Evidence after fix: `openclaw config validate --json` returned valid; `openclaw config get channels.discord.voice.realtime.bootstrapContextFiles --json` returned `["IDENTITY.md","USER.md","SOUL.md"]`; a dry-run config set accepted those three files and rejected `["AGENTS.md"]` with the expected allowed-values error; `openclaw plugins inspect discord --runtime --json` loaded the Discord plugin successfully; `openclaw gateway status --deep --json` returned RPC OK; the helper runtime returned `{ "hasIdentity": true, "hasUser": true, "hasSoul": true, "hasAgents": false }`.

Observed result after fix: The installed runtime accepts the new config, rejects unsupported realtime bootstrap files, loads Discord with the new SDK subpath import, and builds provider instructions containing only the requested profile files.

What was not tested: A fresh live Discord voice conversation recording after enabling the bootstrap files. The behavior is covered by focused unit/e2e tests plus installed-runtime config/plugin/Gateway smoke.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/realtime-bootstrap-context.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts` — 4 files, 60 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.bundle.test.ts` — 2 files, 19 tests passed
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:check-exports`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: feat(discord): follow configured users in voice
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds Discord voice follow-users docs with behavior rules for allowed channels, multi-user handoff, bounded reconciliation, admin moves, and DAVE recovery.
- Preserves follow ownership through DAVE receive recovery and failed handoff cleanup.
- Reworks follow-user reconciliation so large user/guild sets page within a bounded REST lookup budget instead of skipping or starving stale bot cleanup.
- Adds regression coverage for handoff, bounded paging, guild/bot-check rotation, transient REST failures, destroy cleanup, and DAVE recovery ownership.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Discord voice sessions can follow configured users reliably, including large follow lists, multi-user handoff, allowed-channel enforcement, transient reconcile failures, teardown races, and DAVE recovery.
Real environment tested: Mac Studio source checkout running the PR head with the Discord gateway provider enabled.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: configured ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with channels.discord.voice.enabled=true, followUsersEnabled=true, and followUsers=["387380367612706819"].
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm openclaw config validate
Exact steps or command run after this patch: restarted openclaw-gateway-watch-main with pnpm gateway:watch --benchmark from the PR checkout.
Evidence after fix: config validation printed Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.
Evidence after fix: runtime proof printed branch=pr-84264-discord-voice-follow, head=9d520f7209, voice.enabled=true, voice.mode=agent-proxy, voice.followUsersEnabled=true, and voice.followUsers=["387380367612706819"].
Evidence after fix: gateway startup logs on Mac Studio showed OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (9d520f7), [gateway] ready, and the Discord provider starting successfully.
Observed result after fix: Mac Studio is now configured to follow Discord user 387380367612706819 in voice, and the gateway is running the PR code that owns follow handoff, reconciliation, destroy cleanup, transient-error handling, and DAVE recovery behavior.
What was not tested: speaking in a live Discord voice channel; the proof covers real config load, gateway startup, and provider initialization, with behavior covered by focused tests below.

## Verification
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm config:channels:check
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm config:docs:check
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm config:schema:check
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts CHANGELOG.md
Exact steps or command run after this patch: git diff --check
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm build
Exact steps or command run after this patch: codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --uncommitted
Exact steps or command run after this patch: codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review 94ac563399b3ccca6984c90b71ec8bf7da380517..HEAD
Evidence after fix: Discord voice manager e2e suite passed 84/84; Discord config schema test passed 37/37; config generation checks, formatting, diff-check, build, Mac Studio runtime proof, and two autoreview passes, and post-fix check:test-types succeeded.
